page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-48 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4a family. It features 48 dedicated virtual CPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached block storage.

The lack of local NVMe SSDs and dedicated graphics accelerators represents a clear architectural tradeoff, positioning this instance for workloads that rely purely on CPU processing and high memory capacity. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides high memory density per compute unit, allowing memory-bound applications to run without paying for excess CPU cores.

Given the 384.0 GB memory capacity and 48 dedicated vCPUs, this server configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads. Suitable applications include medium-sized in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, caching layers, and data analytics pipelines that require substantial memory footprints but do not require local physical storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-48 is an x86_64 architecture server instance in the c4a family, configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM. This configuration establishes a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, classifying it as a memory-optimized resource. The instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring reliance on network-attached storage for persistent data. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads by providing a dense memory array without requiring the acquisition of excess compute cores. It is designed for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing tasks that do not require hardware acceleration or local physical disks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-48 is an x86_64 server featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384 GB RAM. It lacks local storage and GPUs, making it suitable for memory-intensive database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual server featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and a dense three-hundred-and-eighty-four gigabyte memory configuration.
bullet_points: 384.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators
Requires network-attached storage for data persistence
